alignments.candidate_evidence: Eight years at Google Devices and Services with scaling global commercial operations and two promotions; experience with transformation programs suggests stakeholder management and strategic planning capability at enterprise scope
alignments.job_requirement: Lead deal strategy: Define win themes, stakeholder maps, and executive alignment for complex, multi-threaded pursuits

alignments.candidate_evidence: Product operations background with ownership of requirements, logic design, UAT, rollout, and transformation implies financial/business case modeling and cross-functional negotiation experience
alignments.job_requirement: Structure deal economics: Build ROI/TCO, pricing architectures, and commercial constructs; partner with Deal Desk, Legal, and Risk

alignments.candidate_evidence: Scaling global commercial operations at Google implies regular high-level stakeholder engagement and ability to influence across levels; Strategy & Operations positioning confirms executive-level experience
alignments.job_requirement: Drive executive engagement: Lead C-suite briefings, POVs, and workshops; establish trusted relationships with business and technical stakeholders

alignments.candidate_evidence: Transformation and rollout experience with UAT involvement demonstrates capability to translate requirements, validate feasibility, and manage implementation risks
alignments.job_requirement: Align solution fit: Co-develop high-level solution scope with Solution Architects; confirm feasibility, phase plans, and risks/mitigations

alignments.candidate_evidence: Product Operations background with requirements ownership and logic design directly maps to translating customer needs into product prioritization
alignments.job_requirement: Inform product & roadmap: Synthesize enterprise requirements and market trends; translate into clear use cases and prioritization for Product

alignments.candidate_evidence: Honest gap: product experience comes from ownership, requirements, logic design, UAT, and transformation rather than native engineering or payments domain depth; would require ramp on domain specifics
alignments.job_requirement: Technical fluency (not a pure Solution Architect): Acquiring, issuing, wallets/treasury/FX; comfortable engaging at an architectural level

gaps.gap: No direct payments, financial services, or treasury domain experience; JD emphasizes acquiring, issuing, wallets/treasury/FX as core GTM focus
gaps.mitigation: Domain expertise is learnable via onboarding, customer engagement, and product immersion; candidate's requirements-ownership and solution-architecture background provide the foundational mindset to acquire financial services GTM knowledge quickly

gaps.gap: Limited pre-sales consulting or deal-desk experience; JD prioritizes deal orchestration, RFP/RFI ownership, and commercial terms negotiation with Deal Desk and Legal
gaps.mitigation: Global commercial operations and transformation rollout imply cross-functional negotiation and commercial problem-solving; GTM and deal strategy are accelerated learnings for a strategic operator with executive credibility

gaps.gap: No explicit enterprise quota-carrying or sales-led GTM background mentioned; JD role requires shaping enterprise AE strategy and win plans
gaps.mitigation: Strategy & Operations and Product Operations roles often inform and enable sales strategy without direct sales accountability; can transition into GTM strategy leadership with domain ramp and sales-motion alignment

output.summary: Strong fit to GTM Strategy & Operations role family with eight years of strategy, operations, and commercial-scale transformation at Google. Executive stakeholder engagement, requirements ownership, and cross-functional execution align well to deal strategy and product-roadmap inform duties. Medium gaps in payments domain and pre-sales/deal-desk consulting are manageable with domain ramp and sales-motion alignment. Consider for role; domain fluency and enterprise pre-sales depth are accelerated learnings for a proven strategic operator.
uncertainties: Depth of customer-facing enterprise negotiation and deal-structure experience not fully detailed in candidate profile
Specific exposure to pricing strategy, commercial constructs (usage/SaaS/hybrid), and ROI/TCO modeling not explicitly stated
Extent of hands-on RFP/RFI orchestration and technical validation leadership in previous roles unclear
Career motivation and interest in domain-specific GTM (payments/fintech) not documented
